title: Agency in Language, Alane Suhr | Compile 26
summary: Alane Suhr, an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley, discusses agency in language and argues that humans, not AI systems, hold the agency. The talk covers how language models work, instruction tuning and RLHF, and the gap between the common conception of AI and its technical reality.
writeup: Alane Suhr, an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley, presents a talk at Cursor's Compile 26 arguing that agency in language rests with humans rather than with AI systems. The talk walks through how language models work, covering instruction tuning and RLHF, and contrasts the common conception of AI with its technical reality. The takeaway: the mechanics behind these systems do not support attributing agency to the models themselves.
